headline: Miguel Gomes Unveils Epic War Drama 'Savagery' at Cannes Market
lede: Portuguese auteur Miguel Gomes prepares to launch his most ambitious project yet, bringing his long-gestating war epic to international buyers at this year's Cannes Film Festival.
body:
After years of anticipation, Miguel Gomes is set to introduce his passion project 'Savagery' to the global entertainment marketplace. The Cannes Best Director winner will leverage the prestigious festival's market to spark international interest in his most complex narrative to date.

Gomes, renowned for his innovative storytelling in films like 'Arabian Nights' and 'Grand Tour', has been developing 'Savagery' since 2020. The war drama represents a significant departure from his previous magical realist works, promising a more direct exploration of historical conflict.

Luxbox, the Paris-based sales agency, will spearhead the project's international pre-sales strategy. The agency's track record of championing challenging, artistically rigorous cinema makes them an ideal partner for Gomes' uncompromising vision.

Industry insiders are already speculating about the film's potential festival circuit trajectory. Given Gomes' previous critical acclaim, 'Savagery' stands poised to generate substantial international buzz.
key_facts: Follows Gomes' Cannes Best Director win
War drama genre departure for filmmaker
